description:
GOAL: Driver fn that runs the three single-pass functions to fixpoint with
GROWTH_CAP enforcement, and wire it into ir_codegen::compile() between
ir_lower and ir_typer.

Parent: fz-ul4.11.15. Depends on: fz-ul4.11.15.4.

## Deliverables (extend src/ir_inline.rs)

  /// Iterate the three passes to fixpoint or MAX_ITERATIONS, whichever
  /// comes first. Enforces GROWTH_CAP per fn: track each fn's stmt_count
  /// BEFORE the first iteration; during each rewrite, if the candidate
  /// post-rewrite stmt_count would exceed 4× the pre-pass baseline for
  /// that caller fn, reject THAT individual rewrite (skip the site, continue
  /// with others). Returns total rewrites performed across all iterations.
  pub fn inline_module(m: &mut Module) -> usize;

  Pass order per iteration:
    1. resolve_known_closures_once(m)
    2. inline_calls_once(m)
    3. inline_tail_calls_once(m)

  Rationale: closure-resolve produces Call/TailCall terms; running .15.3/.15.2
  on the same iteration finishes the work.

## GROWTH_CAP implementation

  Before iteration loop:
    let baseline: HashMap<FnId, usize> =
        m.fns.iter().map(|f| (f.id, stmt_count(f))).collect();

  Each rewrite candidate site computes a hypothetical post-stmt-count for the
  caller (caller_baseline + callee_stmts). If > 4 * baseline[caller.id],
  reject this site. Per-site rejection — does NOT halt the iteration.

  Note: baseline is fixed at the start; we do NOT recompute it after each
  iteration. This means the cap is "4× of the ORIGINAL fn size", not
  "4× of last iteration." Intentional: prevents 4 × 4 × 4 = 64× blowup
  across iterations.

## Pipeline integration

  src/ir_codegen.rs::compile() current sequence (post-.11.24):
    let mut module = module.clone();
    let types = ir_typer::type_module(&module);
    ir_typer::rewrite_vec_kinds(&mut module, &types);
    let types = ir_typer::type_module(&module);
    // ... per-fn compile loop ...

  New sequence:
    let mut module = module.clone();
    let _inline_count = ir_inline::inline_module(&mut module);
    let types = ir_typer::type_module(&module);
    ir_typer::rewrite_vec_kinds(&mut module, &types);
    let types = ir_typer::type_module(&module);
    // ... per-fn compile loop ...

  Also add `mod ir_inline;` in src/main.rs (after `mod ir_codegen;`).

## Tests

  - Chain inline: hand-built A tail-calls B tail-calls C, all leaves; after
    inline_module, A's body has no TailCall (or at most one TailCall, if
    C tail-calls D and D is non-leaf).
  - Fixpoint terminates: pathological case (no inlinable sites) returns 0.
  - MAX_ITERATIONS cap: synthetic test that demonstrates the loop stops at 3
    rounds even when more inlining is theoretically possible.
  - GROWTH_CAP engages: caller with 4 stmts; callee with 5 stmts; 10
    callsites in caller; without cap caller would balloon to 4+50=54 stmts
    (13.5× growth). With cap=4 (max 16 stmts), only the first few sites
    inline; remaining sites left as Call/TailCall.
  - End-to-end: run main.rs SAMPLE program through `fz run` test harness;
    assert same printed output as pre-inline baseline. Tests count of 345+
    must not regress.

## Risks

  - Inserting inliner BEFORE typer means typer sees inlined bodies. The
    typer is flow-sensitive but provenance-agnostic, so semantically equivalent
    code should produce equivalent types. Mitigation: full test suite must
    pass with zero regressions.
  - rewrite_vec_kinds runs AFTER inlining and AFTER typing. If inlining
    introduces new MakeVec literals (it doesn't — alpha_rename preserves
    Prim::MakeVec verbatim), the second type pass handles them. No expected
    issue.

## Acceptance

  - cargo test passes (no regressions in 345+ test suite).
  - New inliner-driver tests pass.
  - `fz run` still works on SAMPLE.
  - One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.15.5.

## References

  - src/ir_codegen.rs::compile() (line 1470+ for current sequence).
  - fz-ul4.11.24.5 (rewrite_vec_kinds pipeline structure) for the
    clone-then-re-type pattern this extends.
title: Inliner fixpoint + bloat cap + pipeline integration
